creator: dougb@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, 19 Sep 2007, Wesley Shields wrote:

> On Tue, Sep 18, 2007 at 11:19:14PM -0700, Doug Barton wrote:
>> In discussion with Kris about this idea he expressed a concern about
>> including this information in +CONTENTS, preferring instead that it be
>> included in /var/db/ports/$name since information about the distfiles
>> is relevant to the ports, not the packages.
>
> I think Kris' concern is valid and I think this is a much better idea
> than what was initially proposed in this PR.  I do have some thoughts
> though:
>
> Your patch doesn't work for me.  Here's the output of trying it out in a
> jail on archivers/unzip:

It's missing a mkdir -p (as I discovered myself in later testing on virgin 
stuff). Your new patch adds that, which looks good. Thanks for catching 
this.

> Should we do anything on deinstall of a port?  It might be nice to
> remove ${DISTFILE_LIST} and ${PORT_DBDIR}/${UNIQUENAME} if it is empty.

If we are deinstalling I think that removing DISTFILE_LIST should be done 
whether it's empty or not. Removing the directory if empty is a good thing 
too.

> I've attached a patch which works for me (tested with archivers/unzip
> and ports-mgmt/portmaster).  It also touches the rmconfig target which
> will only remove ${PORT_DBDIR}/${UNIQUENAME} if it is empty.  In the
> case of using this patch with ports-mgmt/portmaster we get a zero length
> distfiles file.  I think this is acceptable and the tools which will
> parse this file must be able to handle this case.

On cursory review it looks good to me. The code I have in my current dev 
version of portmaster does "grep ^DISTFILE" to handle this, and the idea 
of putting comments in the file. I'll also look at adding the removal of 
the file and directory as discussed above.

>> I am not likely to have time to rework the pkg_* parts of the original
>> patch any time soon unfortunately. Wesley, if you're still interested
>> in this topic you might want to have a go ...
>>
>> Doug
>
> I don't think modifying the pkg_* tools in the base makes any sense if
> we are going to be moving it to /var/db/ports.  I think modifications to
> things which work on ports (portmaster, portsclean, etc) will be
> necessary after this change is made.  I'll be preparing patches for
> portsclean if this change is committed.

I still liked the idea of being able to add the display of the distfiles 
to the tools, especially pkg_info. But since I'm not able to do the work 
right now, I'll leave it at that. :)

Doug

-- 

     This .signature sanitized for your protection
